symptoms.symptom_name: Tight cluster of vesicles on an erythematous base with early honey-coloured crusting on the cheek

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Grouped tense vesicles on an erythematous base evolving to shallow ulcers and crust is the characteristic morphology of HSV reactivation.
Early golden/honey crusting overlaps with impetigo, but the intact grouped vesicles and prodrome preceding the crust favour HSV; secondary bacterial infection remains possible.

symptoms.symptom_name: 48-hour neuropathic (tingling/burning) prodrome at the site before the blisters

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A localised tingling/burning prodrome preceding grouped vesicles reflects viral travel down the sensory nerve and is characteristic of herpes reactivation.
A neuropathic prodrome is not a feature of contact dermatitis, which is typically itchy without a preceding nerve-type sensation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Periocular location — eruption on the infraorbital cheek close to the lower eyelid margin

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The proximity of active HSV to the ocular surface is the single feature that converts a benign cold sore into a sight-threat, via direct extension or autoinoculation; it drives the urgent in-person eye-assessment disposition.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent stress and UV/sun exposure with a past history of recurrent cold sores

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Psychological stress and ultraviolet exposure are well-recognised triggers of HSV reactivation, and a prior cold-sore history establishes latent HSV.

symptoms.symptom_name: No current ocular symptoms by patient report (eye not red/painful, vision normal, no photophobia or gritty sensation)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative patient-reported eye screen lowers the immediate probability of established keratitis but cannot exclude an early dendritic ulcer, which can be near-asymptomatic and needs fluorescein/slit-lamp — hence a mandatory safety-net rather than reassurance.

symptoms.symptom_name: V2-only distribution with mild non-neuralgic pain and no forehead/scalp/tip-of-nose lesions

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The cheek (V2) distribution, absence of forehead/scalp/upper-eyelid or Hutchinson-sign (nasal-tip) lesions and mild rather than deep neuralgic pain argue against V1 zoster, though it cannot be fully excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, facial/eyelid swelling or rapidly spreading erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever, spreading erythema, eyelid swelling and painful/restricted eye movements makes preseptal/orbital cellulitis unlikely now, though it remains a mandatory safety-net for a periocular skin infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician basic ophthalmic examination — visual acuity grossly intact, no conjunctival injection, no corneal clouding, no dendritic ulcer on gross inspection, painless normal extraocular movements

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person examination: a grossly normal eye examination lowers the probability of established keratitis, but gross inspection without fluorescein/slit-lamp cannot exclude an early dendritic ulcer. Cannot be performed over telehealth — the basis for the mandatory urgent in-person eye assessment.
Sealed in-person finding: tender regional lymphadenopathy accompanies an active localised viral skin infection. Cannot be palpated over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals (BP 132/84, HR 88, Temp 37.3, RR 16, SpO2 99%, Wt 74 kg)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed in-person measurement; afebrile, haemodynamically stable vitals argue against systemic bacterial spread. Not obtainable in telehealth without a home device.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent same-day in-person ophthalmic assessment — visual acuity, slit-lamp and fluorescein staining for a corneal dendritic ulcer (GP with eye-examination capacity or ophthalmology)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The one assessment telehealth cannot perform and the crux of this case: fluorescein/slit-lamp examination is what excludes an early herpes simplex keratitis that can be near-asymptomatic; it must be arranged the same day given active periocular HSV.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person review of the skin lesion to confirm HSV morphology and proximity to the eyelid margin
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms the grouped-vesicle morphology, distance from the lid margin and any early bacterial superinfection, and accompanies the eye examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab (HSV PCR) of the lesion base
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Can confirm HSV if the diagnosis is uncertain, but should not delay antiviral therapy or the urgent eye assessment; the clinical picture is characteristic.
No systemic features and no established cellulitis — this is a viral eruption; antibiotics and a septic screen would be over-investigation unless secondary bacterial cellulitis develops.
